cardinal_sin_flags.quote: The state government rejected their declaration without examining the underlying claim.
cardinal_sin_flags.why: We only know that the state government (via MB press statements) did not PUBLICLY ADDRESS the misconduct claims in their rejection, focusing instead on Mubarak's lack of legal standing. Asserting they rejected it 'without examining' assumes knowledge of government's internal administrative/investigative processes.

verification_log.claim: All four Undangs of Negeri Sembilan issued declaration deposing Tuanku Muhriz and naming Tunku Nadzaruddin Tuanku Ja'afar
Article X(1)(b) UUTKNS 1959 refers to collective Undang action
verification_log.primary_source: Laws of the Constitution of NS 1959 (ns.gov.my)

verification_log.claim: 1967 bypass of Tuanku Muhriz at age 19
DKU presided by Tuanku Muhriz confirmed dismissal 17 Apr 2026
Four Undangs cited alleged royal misconduct from 5 Mar 2026 inquiry
Telaga Undang speaker Sarifah Norzaidah Al-Qadri called act 'menderhaka'
verification_log.primary_source: Sinar Harian / The Rakyat Post 20 Apr 2026

overall_assessment: Highly factual, structurally sound, accurately incorporates complexities of breaking news. By restoring the context of the other three Undangs' involvement, it correctly elevates narrative from dismissed individual's lone stunt to a genuine, untested constitutional dispute. Only flaw: minor epistemic overclaim regarding state government 'not examining' underlying misconduct claims, when observable reality is they did not address those claims publicly.
